walkthrough.title: Place the Main Board
walkthrough.text: Let's get Scholars of the South Tigris set up. First, place the Main Board in the middle of the play area where everyone can reach it. This is the large board featuring the Map with 12 circular Spots around its perimeter, the House of Wisdom with 9 Card spaces arranged in 3 Guild columns, 6 Research Tracks along the bottom, 8 Translator Deck spaces along the top, and 4 Caliph Card spaces in the top-right corner.

walkthrough.title: Place the Translator Decks
walkthrough.text: Find the 64 Translator Cards. These come in 8 separate Decks of 8 Cards each, and every Deck features the same character illustration on every card. Shuffle each Deck separately, then place each Deck into its indicated space along the top of the Main Board so that the artwork lines up across the Decks. Only the top card of each Deck is accessible during play.

walkthrough.title: Deal Out Scroll Cards
walkthrough.text: Shuffle all 48 Scroll Cards together into one face-down pile. Now place 1 Scroll Card face-up onto each of the 6 spaces around the Map. Then place 1 Scroll Card face-up onto each of the top 3 spaces in the House of Wisdom (one per Guild column, in the highest row). Keep the remaining shuffled Scroll Cards set aside for now -- you will use them to build the Draw Pile in a later step.

walkthrough.title: Set Up Starting Translators
walkthrough.text: Find the 10 Starting Translator Cards. These are distinct from the 64 regular Translator Cards. Shuffle them and reveal 4 at random. Return the other 6 to the box. Place each of the 4 revealed Starting Translators into their indicated Room along the bottom of the Main Board. Each Starting Translator and each Room has a white number against a red background that tells you which Room it belongs to.

walkthrough.title: Deal Resource Cards
walkthrough.text: Find the 10 Resource Cards. Shuffle them and reveal 1 face-up below each of the 4 Starting Translators you just placed. Return the remaining Resource Cards to the box. Each Resource Card shows a sequence of bonuses that players will resolve during the draft step later.

walkthrough.title: Give Each Player Their Components
walkthrough.text: Each player picks a colour and takes all items in that colour: 7 Action Cards, 20 Influence tokens, 6 Research Markers, 1 Player Marker, and 1 Bag. Also give each player 1 Player Board. Make sure everyone has all 20 Influence in their supply -- you will be using these throughout the game to mark ownership of Translators, Scroll Cards, and Guilds.

walkthrough.title: Place Research Markers and Player Markers
walkthrough.text: Have all players place 1 Research Marker of their colour on the 0 space of each of the 6 Research Tracks on the Main Board. That uses all 6 markers per player. Each player should also place their Player Marker on the top-centre space of the Map. Stacking order does not matter when multiple markers share a space.

walkthrough.title: Place the Neutral Marker
walkthrough.text: Find the single Neutral Marker. Place it on the bottom-centre space of the Map. This marker moves clockwise around the Map during Rest turns, skipping White Spots and only stopping on Black Spots. It determines which Scroll Cards may be displaced or delivered to the House of Wisdom when players Rest.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Main Supply
walkthrough.text: Place all Dice, Workers, Gold, and Silver into a shared Main Supply within reach of all players. The game includes 100 opaque Dice (40 white, 20 each in red, blue, and yellow), 15 translucent Dice (5 each in purple, orange, and green), 56 Workers (20 white, 12 each in red, blue, and yellow), 35 Gold, and 50 Silver. Gold, Silver, and Workers are unlimited -- if they run out, use a suitable replacement.

walkthrough.title: Determine First Player
walkthrough.text: Randomly determine a starting player and give them the First Player Marker. This marker stays with the same player for the entire game -- it does not rotate. Play will proceed clockwise from this player on every turn.

walkthrough.title: Choose Casual or Epic Mode
walkthrough.text: As a group, decide whether to play the Casual or Epic version of the game. For first-time players, especially at higher player counts, the Casual version is recommended. It does not change any rules -- it simply removes a few Scroll Cards from the Draw Pile to ensure a slightly shorter game.

walkthrough.title: Build the Scroll Card Draw Pile
walkthrough.text: Shuffle all 4 Caliph Cards face-down. Find the Caliph Setup Card and the remaining Scroll Cards you set aside earlier. Using the Setup Card as a guide for your player count, create 6 separate stacks of cards. For the Casual game, the top stack always has 3 Scroll Cards and the bottom stack has 1 Scroll Card. Each of the 4 middle stacks gets 2 to 4 Scroll Cards (based on player count) plus 1 random Caliph Card, shuffled together. Stack all 6 piles on top of one another in the correct order to form the Draw Pile, then place it to the left of the Main Board alongside the Map. Set any unused Scroll Cards aside as a reserve -- they may be needed in the final rounds. Return the Caliph Setup Card to the box.

walkthrough.title: Deal Goal Cards
walkthrough.text: Shuffle the 6 Goal Cards and deal 1 to each player. Return the rest to the box. Each player places their Goal Card on the right-most space of their Player Board. On the top space of the Goal Card, place a White Worker. Below that, place the indicated coloured Worker. On the bottom space, place the indicated secondary Die. These provide starting resources and give each player personal targets to work toward during the game.

walkthrough.title: Distribute Starting Scroll Cards
walkthrough.text: Give each player their Starting Scroll Card. It has the same character illustration on its reverse side as the one on their Goal Card. Return any unclaimed Starting Scroll Cards to the box. Players place their Starting Scroll Card face-up above their Player Board. This Starting Scroll converts every 2 Silver into 1 Gold at game end, scores 1 VP for every 2 Gold you have, and loses you 3 VP if you have 6 or more White Dice.

walkthrough.title: Draft Starting Translators
walkthrough.text: In reverse turn order (counter-clockwise, starting with the player to the right of the First Player), each player selects a Starting Translator and Resource Card pair. When choosing, place 1 of your Influence on the top-left corner of your chosen Starting Translator to mark ownership. Then take the Resource Card below it and resolve its icons from top to bottom: add the indicated Dice to your Bag, draw 4 Dice at random from your Bag and roll them, gain the indicated Gold, gain the indicated Silver, gain the indicated Workers, and resolve any other printed bonus. After resolving, flip the Resource Card over and place it on your Player Board as a Player Aid. Any unselected Translators remain as Neutral Translators (no Influence on them). Return unselected Resource Cards to the box.

walkthrough.title: Ready to Play
walkthrough.text: Setup is complete. Each player should have their Player Board with a Goal Card and Starting Scroll Card, some Dice in hand, some Gold and Silver, some Workers, a partially filled Bag, 7 Action Cards in hand, and Influence in their supply. The Main Board should have Translator Decks along the top, 4 Starting Translators in Rooms along the bottom, Scroll Cards on the Map and in the House of Wisdom, Research Markers on the tracks, and the Draw Pile alongside the Map. You are ready to begin.

Flip Resource Card as Player Aid; unselected Translators become Neutral

summary.title: Ready to Play
bullets: Each player has: Player Board, Goal Card, Starting Scroll, Dice in hand, Gold, Silver, Workers, 7 Action Cards, Influence supply, Bag with Dice
Main Board has: Translator Decks, 4 Starting Translators in Rooms, Scroll Cards on Map and House of Wisdom, Draw Pile alongside Map

walkthrough.title: Game Flow Overview
walkthrough.text: Scholars of the South Tigris is played over an undetermined number of Rounds. Every Round, each player takes a single turn, starting with the First Player and going clockwise. This continues round after round until all 4 Caliph Cards have been revealed. When that happens, finish the current Round, then play 1 final Round. After that, scoring takes place. On each turn, you must choose to either Work or Rest.

walkthrough.title: Working: Overview
walkthrough.text: When you choose to Work, you follow three steps. First, place 1 Action Card from your hand onto an empty Action Slot on your Player Board. You start with 5 Action Slots but can unlock a 6th by completing your Goal Card. Second, place 1 or 2 Dice from your hand onto that Action Card. Third, optionally place Workers to alter your Dice, then resolve available actions in any order. Important rule: you can never use Dice on the same turn that you gain them to your hand.

walkthrough.title: Action Colour and Value
walkthrough.text: When Working, your Dice and Workers create a colour and value for your main action. The action's value ranges from 1 to 12, and you may always underspend. A single Die creates an action of that Die's colour and face value. When you play 2 Dice, coloured Dice take priority over White Dice. Two primary-colour Dice mix to create a secondary colour -- for example, a Red Die plus a Blue Die makes a Purple action. If the mix could produce two different secondary colours, you choose. Secondary Dice always take priority over primary Dice.

walkthrough.title: Using Workers on Dice
walkthrough.text: Before resolving your main action, you may place up to 2 Workers per Die on your Action Card. A Worker does one of two things. If the Worker matches the Die's colour, it increases that Die to a 6. If the Worker is a different colour, it changes the Die's colour to the Worker's colour. Important: you cannot place 2 coloured Workers on a single Die to create a secondary colour. Workers placed on Action Cards are returned to the Main Supply when you Rest. Workers spent as a cost during an action come from your personal supply.

walkthrough.title: Action Slots and Action Card Effects
walkthrough.text: Each Action Slot on your Player Board grants access to 1 of the 4 main actions: Recruit, Travel, Research, or Translate. Some Action Slots offer a choice of 2 actions. Your Action Cards also have their own effects. Blue-background effects are Rest effects, resolved when you Rest later. Brown-background effects are immediate small actions that can be resolved in any order alongside your main action during the turn you play the card. These small actions include things like gaining Workers, drawing Dice, Influencing Scroll Cards, gaining Silver, or placing/removing Gold on Translators.

walkthrough.title: The Recruit Action
walkthrough.text: When Recruiting, you either Dispatch or Employ 1 Translator from the top section of the Main Board. Colour does not matter for Recruit -- only the action's value. Each adjacent pair of Translators requires a minimum value: the leftmost pair needs a value of just 1, and the rightmost pair requires 7 or more. Dispatching is free: resolve the effects in the top-right corner of the Translator Card, then move it to the bottom of its Deck. Employing costs 0 to 4 Silver, depending on the Room where you place the Translator. Flip the card over, place it in an available Room, pay the Room's Silver cost, resolve the Room's immediate effects, and place 1 of your Influence on the top-left corner to mark ownership. If all Rooms are full, you cannot Employ.

walkthrough.title: The Travel Action
walkthrough.text: When Traveling, move your Player Marker clockwise around the Map. There are 12 steps around the Map. You must move at least 1 step, and with a 12-valued action you could do a full loop. The colour of your action provides a small bonus: if you cross the path matching your action's colour, you gain either Influence on a Scroll Card (primary colours) or 2 Silver (secondary colours). White Spots provide smaller benefits like gaining Silver or drawing Dice. Black Spots are where you can Deliver Scroll Cards to the House of Wisdom.

walkthrough.title: Delivering Scroll Cards
walkthrough.text: To Deliver, move to a Black Spot on the Map. Pick up the adjacent Scroll Card and place it into an available Card space in one of the 3 Guilds in the House of Wisdom. Guilds must be filled from top to bottom. Pay 4 Silver. Gain 1 or 2 Influence in that Guild (as shown on the Card space). Gain 1 Gold; also gain 1 White Die to your Bag if you placed the Scroll in the lowest row. Move up 1 space on the Research Track matching the Scroll Card's type. Finally, draw and reveal the top card of the Draw Pile. If it is a Scroll Card, place it into the empty Map space. If it is a Caliph Card, resolve it immediately. If all 9 House of Wisdom spaces are full, you cannot Deliver.

walkthrough.title: The Research Action
walkthrough.text: When Researching, you choose 1 of 4 options printed along the top of the Research Tracks. The 1-plus option can be any colour and any value: gain 1 Worker and 1 Silver. The 4-plus option requires a colour and value of at least 4: spend 1 Gold to move 1 space up the matching Research Track, and gain 2 Silver. The 8-plus option requires a colour and value of at least 8: move 1 space up the matching track, and gain 2 Silver, at no Gold cost. The 10-plus option requires a colour and value of at least 10: spend 2 Gold to move 2 spaces up the matching track, and gain 2 Silver.

walkthrough.title: Research Tracks
walkthrough.text: There are 6 Research Tracks, one for each colour: Physics (Red), Mathematics (Purple), Astronomy (Blue), Chemistry (Orange), Philosophy (Yellow), and Biology (Green). Each track goes from 0 to 7. Odd-numbered spaces (transparent) give an immediate benefit when you land on them. Even-numbered spaces (coloured) provide income only when you Rest. Gaining movement beyond level 7 does not move your marker further -- it instead earns you either 1 Influence in a Guild or 1 Gold, as shown at the top of each track.

walkthrough.title: The Translate Action
walkthrough.text: When Translating, you convert a Scroll Card in the House of Wisdom from its original language into Arabic using the Employed Translators in the Rooms. The colour of your action must match the Guild where the Scroll Card sits -- for example, a Green action for the Green Guild. The value does not matter. Languages are in 3 tiers: Tier 1 is Persian and Syriac, Tier 2 is Greek and Hebrew, Tier 3 is Chinese and Sanskrit. Arabic is Tier 0. You must forge a line of Translators from the Scroll's language to Arabic. Place 1 Gold from your supply onto each Translator used. Pay 1 Silver to the owner of each Translator that is not yours. For Neutral Translators, pay 1 Silver to the Main Supply. You cannot use the same language twice in a single translation chain.

walkthrough.title: Completing a Translation
walkthrough.text: After forging the translation chain, take the Scroll Card and place it above your Player Board. Move 1 space up on the Research Track matching the Scroll's type. Resolve any immediate effects printed on the Scroll Card itself. Then slide up any remaining Scroll Cards in the House of Wisdom to fill empty spaces, and Retire any Translators that have met their Gold requirement. This is the primary way to score Victory Points from Scroll Cards at game end.

walkthrough.title: Retiring Translators
walkthrough.text: A Translator Retires when the number of Gold on it reaches the number shown above its Room. When an Employed Translator Retires: return all Gold to the Main Supply, return the Influence to the owner's supply, and the owner tucks the Translator Card under 1 of their 4 brown Action Slots. Retirees can never sit below the black Action Slots. If this is the first Retiree under that Action Slot, there is no cost. For each additional Retiree already under that slot, pay 1 Silver to the Main Supply or gain 1 White Die to your Bag. When a Neutral Translator Retires, return its Gold and Card to its Deck. Translators only Retire at the very end of the current player's turn.

walkthrough.title: Resting
walkthrough.text: Instead of Working, you may Rest. You must have at least 1 Action Card on your Player Board. Resting has 4 steps. Step 1: Draw and reveal the bottom card of the Scroll Card Draw Pile. If it is a Scroll Card, the Neutral Marker moves clockwise around the Map a number of steps equal to the Scroll's VP value, skipping White Spots. Then either the newly revealed Scroll or the Map Scroll adjacent to the Neutral Marker is placed into an empty Guild in the House of Wisdom (if available) or discarded. Step 2: Gain Income from all Rest icons on your Player Board, resolved left to right. This includes harvesting Research Tracks -- you gain the benefit of the highest even-numbered space your marker has reached. Step 3: Return all Dice from your Player Board to your Bag. Step 4: Return all Action Cards to your hand, sending Workers on them back to the Main Supply.

walkthrough.title: Influence
walkthrough.text: Influence is used in 4 ways. First, to mark ownership when Employing Translators. Second, to Influence Scroll Cards on the Map or in the House of Wisdom -- opponents who want to Deliver or Translate a Scroll with your Influence on it must pay you 1 Silver. Third, to Influence Guilds: the first player to place Influence takes control, shown by placing at the top of the Minaret. A player takes control when they have more Influence than the current controller. Fourth, to claim rewards on Caliph Cards when they are revealed. Influence is limited to 20 per player. If you have none in supply, you may move 1 from a Scroll Card or Guild (not from a Translator).

walkthrough.title: Caliph Cards
walkthrough.text: Caliph Cards can be revealed when Delivering Scroll Cards or when Resting. When revealed: place the Caliph Card into the left-most empty Caliph space, resolve any event tied to that space, then the controllers of each Guild (Purple, Orange, Green -- left to right) move 1 Influence from their Guild to an available space on the Caliph Card. The top space is always worth 3 VP. The middle space is worth 1 VP plus a Die. The bottom space offers either Retiring a specific Translator (at the cost of gaining 1 White Die) or gaining 1 Gold. Non-controlling players with Influence in a Guild gain a consolation reward. The 2nd, 3rd, and 4th Caliph Cards trigger events: 1 Gold placed on each Starting Translator, or a Neutral Translator added to the left-most empty Room.

walkthrough.title: Drawing Dice
walkthrough.text: Whenever you draw Dice or add new Dice to your hand, you must roll them. Dice must always be drawn randomly from your Bag -- you cannot choose. If your Bag is empty when you need to draw, return all Dice from your Player Board to your Bag (except those from your current action), then continue drawing. If still empty after that, you simply do not draw further. White and primary Dice are standard 6-sided (1-6). Secondary Dice only have numbers 4, 5, and 6 (two of each). You may look in your own Bag at any time, but not your opponents' Bags.

walkthrough.title: End of Game and Scoring
walkthrough.text: The game ends when all 4 Caliph Cards have been revealed. Finish the current Round, then play 1 final Round. At the end, take any Gold still on your Employed Translators and add it to your supply. Calculate your Dice Sum: total coloured Dice minus total White Dice. Players are ranked on the Dice Sum banner for VP. Final scoring has 5 categories: VP from Dice Sum ranking, VP printed on Retired Translators, VP from Guilds you control plus Caliph Card Influence, VP from Research Track positions (1 VP at levels 1-2, 2 VP at 3-4, 3 VP at 5, 4 VP at 6, 5 VP at 7 for each track), and VP from Translated Scroll Cards. Highest total VP wins. Tiebreaker: most Employed Translators still on the board, then most Workers in supply.

summary.title: Game Flow Overview
bullets: Played over an undetermined number of Rounds; each player takes 1 turn per Round (clockwise)
Game end triggered when all 4 Caliph Cards are revealed
Finish current Round + 1 final Round, then score
On your turn: choose to Work or Rest

summary.title: Working: Overview
bullets: Step 1: Place 1 Action Card from hand onto an empty Action Slot (5 slots, unlock 6th via Goal Card)
Step 2: Place 1 or 2 Dice from hand onto the Action Card
Step 3: Place Workers to alter Dice, then resolve actions in any order
Cannot use Dice gained the same turn

summary.title: Action Colour and Value
bullets: Actions range from value 1-12; you may underspend
1 coloured Die = that colour; coloured Die + White Die = coloured Die's colour
2 primary Dice mix to secondary (Red+Blue=Purple, Red+Yellow=Orange, Blue+Yellow=Green)
Secondary Dice take priority over primary; choose between 2 possible secondaries

summary.title: Using Workers on Dice
bullets: Up to 2 Workers per Die
Same-colour Worker = increase Die to 6
Different-colour Worker = change Die colour
Cannot use 2 coloured Workers on 1 Die to create secondary
Workers on Action Cards return to Main Supply when you Rest

summary.title: Action Slots and Action Card Effects
bullets: Each Action Slot provides 1 of 4 main actions: Recruit, Travel, Research, Translate
Some slots offer a choice between 2 actions
Blue-background effects on Action Cards = Rest income (delayed)
Brown-background effects = immediate small actions on the turn played

summary.title: The Recruit Action
bullets: Colour does not matter; only value matters
Dispatch: free -- resolve top-right corner effects, return card to bottom of Deck
Employ: pay 0-4 Silver for Room, flip card, place in Room, resolve Room effects, mark with 1 Influence
Adjacent Translator pairs require minimum values: leftmost pair = 1+, rightmost = 7+

summary.title: The Travel Action
bullets: Move Player Marker clockwise around the Map (12 steps total)
Must move at least 1 step; value 12 = full loop
Crossing path matching action colour = bonus (primary: Influence Scroll; secondary: 2 Silver)
White Spots = small benefits; Black Spots = Deliver Scroll Cards

summary.title: Delivering Scroll Cards
bullets: Move to Black Spot, pick up adjacent Scroll, place into House of Wisdom Guild (top to bottom)
Pay 4 Silver; gain 1-2 Influence in that Guild; gain 1 Gold (+ 1 White Die to Bag if lowest row)
Move up 1 on matching Research Track; reveal top card of Draw Pile
Cannot Deliver if all 9 House of Wisdom spaces are full

summary.title: The Research Action
bullets: 1+: any colour/value -- gain 1 Worker + 1 Silver
4+: colour required, spend 1 Gold -- move 1 space on matching track + 2 Silver
8+: colour required, no Gold cost -- move 1 space on matching track + 2 Silver
10+: colour required, spend 2 Gold -- move 2 spaces on matching track + 2 Silver

summary.title: Research Tracks
bullets: 6 tracks: Physics (Red), Mathematics (Purple), Astronomy (Blue), Chemistry (Orange), Philosophy (Yellow), Biology (Green)
Levels 0-7; odd spaces = immediate benefit; even spaces = Rest income
Beyond level 7: gain 1 Influence in a Guild or 1 Gold instead

summary.title: The Translate Action
bullets: Action colour must match the Guild of the target Scroll; value does not matter
Languages: Tier 1 (Persian, Syriac), Tier 2 (Greek, Hebrew), Tier 3 (Chinese, Sanskrit), Arabic = Tier 0
Forge a line of Translators from Scroll language to Arabic; place 1 Gold on each
Pay 1 Silver per non-owned Translator; cannot reuse a language in the chain

summary.title: Completing a Translation
bullets: Take Scroll Card, place above Player Board
Move 1 space on matching Research Track; resolve Scroll's immediate effects
Slide up remaining Scroll Cards in House of Wisdom to fill gaps
Retire any Translators that met their Gold requirement

summary.title: Retiring Translators
bullets: Retire when Gold on Translator equals Room's Gold requirement
Employed: return Gold to supply, return Influence to owner, tuck under 1 of 4 brown Action Slots
First Retiree under a slot = free; each additional = 1 Silver or gain 1 White Die to Bag
Neutral: return Gold + Card to Deck; Translators only Retire at end of current player's turn

summary.title: Resting
bullets: Must have at least 1 Action Card on Player Board
Step 1: Reveal bottom of Draw Pile -- Scroll moves Neutral Marker (VP value steps, skip White Spots), then deliver or discard
Step 2: Gain Income left to right; harvest Research Tracks (highest even-numbered space reached)
Step 3: Return all Dice from Player Board to Bag
Step 4: Return all Action Cards to hand, Workers on them to Main Supply

summary.title: Influence
bullets: 4 uses: mark Translator ownership, Influence Scroll Cards (opponents pay 1 Silver to interact), control Guilds (majority), claim Caliph Card spaces
Limited to 20 per player; if empty, may relocate from a Scroll Card or Guild (not Translator)
Guild control: most Influence = control; first to place takes top of Minaret

summary.title: Caliph Cards
bullets: Revealed during Deliver or Rest; place in left-most Caliph space
Guild controllers (Purple, Orange, Green) each move 1 Influence to Caliph Card
Top space = 3 VP; middle = 1 VP + Die; bottom = Retire a Translator or 1 Gold
Events on 2nd-4th cards: Gold on Starting Translators, or add Neutral Translator to Rooms

summary.title: Drawing Dice
bullets: Always draw randomly from Bag and roll
If Bag empty: return all Dice from Player Board to Bag (except current action), then draw
Primary Dice (opaque): faces 1-6; Secondary Dice (translucent): faces 4-6 only
May look in own Bag anytime; never look in opponents' Bags

summary.title: End of Game and Scoring
bullets: Triggered when all 4 Caliph Cards revealed; finish Round + 1 final Round
Dice Sum: coloured Dice minus White Dice -- ranked on banner for VP
Score: Dice Sum VP + Retired Translator VP + Guild control/Caliph VP + Research Track VP (1-5 VP per track based on level) + Scroll Card VP
Tiebreaker: most Employed Translators on board, then most Workers in supply

walkthrough.title: Welcome to the Tutorial
walkthrough.text: In this practice tutorial, we will walk through a scripted first few turns of Scholars of the South Tigris with 3 players: Red, Blue, and Yellow. We recommend 3 players for a learning game -- it keeps the play area manageable while still showing off player interaction like paying Silver to use opponents' Translators and competing for Guild control. Follow along with the moves, even if your player count differs.

walkthrough.title: Starting Position
walkthrough.text: After setup, each player should have 7 Action Cards in hand, some Dice in hand from their Resource Card draft (usually 4 Dice), some Gold and Silver, some Workers, and a partially-filled Bag. Each player owns 1 Starting Translator with their Influence on it, has a Goal Card on their Player Board with a White Worker, a coloured Worker, and a secondary Die on it, and a Starting Scroll Card above their board. All 6 Research Markers sit at level 0. Remember: Yellow goes first (they have the First Player Marker), then Red, then Blue.

walkthrough.title: Yellow's First Turn: Work with Recruit
walkthrough.text: Yellow looks at their hand: they have a Red 3, a White 4, a Blue 2, and a Yellow 5. They want to Employ a Translator to start building their translation chain. Yellow places an Action Card on their Recruit Action Slot. They place their Yellow 5 Die on the card, giving them a Yellow 5 action. This lets them access the first 3 pairs of Translators (which need values 1, 4, and 5). They find a Persian/Arabic Translator and choose to Employ it. The available Room costs 2 Silver -- Yellow pays 2 Silver, flips the Translator, places it in the Room, resolves the Room's immediate effect (gaining 1 Silver back), and places 1 Influence on the Translator. Nice -- Yellow now has a direct Persian-to-Arabic translation line.

walkthrough.title: Why That Was Smart
walkthrough.text: Employing a Translator early is one of the strongest opening moves. Yellow now has 2 Translators -- their Starting Translator and the newly Employed one. The Persian/Arabic Translator is especially valuable because Persian is a Tier 1 language, meaning Yellow only needs 1 Translator (plus 1 Gold) to translate any Persian Scroll Card. Every Translator you own also avoids the 1 Silver fee your opponents would pay to use it.

walkthrough.title: Red's First Turn: Work with Travel
walkthrough.text: Red decides to Travel. They place an Action Card on their Travel Action Slot. They have a Green 5 (a translucent secondary Die), so they place it on the card. A Green 5 means Red can move up to 5 steps clockwise. Red counts the spots and decides to move 4 steps to land on a White Spot that lets them draw 1 Die from their Bag. Along the way, Red crosses the Green path, so they gain the secondary-colour bonus of 2 Silver. After landing, Red draws 1 Die from their Bag and rolls it. Red also resolves the Action Card's brown-background effect: gaining 1 Worker of any colour from the Main Supply.

walkthrough.title: Blue's First Turn: Work with Research
walkthrough.text: Blue wants to advance on a Research Track early. They have a Red 4 and a White 2 in hand. Blue places an Action Card on their Research Action Slot, then places both Dice -- the Red 4 and White 2. With a coloured Die and a White Die, the action is Red 6 (coloured takes priority; 4+2=6). Blue chooses the 4-plus Research option: spend 1 Gold to move 1 space up the Physics (Red) Research Track, and gain 2 Silver. Blue pays 1 Gold, moves their Red Research Marker from 0 to 1, gains the immediate benefit of the level 1 space (if any), and collects 2 Silver.

walkthrough.title: Yellow's Second Turn: The Mistake -- Wasting a High Die
walkthrough.text: Yellow is excited and wants to do something big. They have a White 4 and a Blue 2 left in hand. Yellow places an Action Card on their Travel Action Slot and puts down just the White 4. A White 4 means Yellow can move up to 4 spaces -- but White actions do not trigger the colour-path bonus. Yellow moves 3 steps to a Black Spot and wants to Deliver a Scroll Card to the House of Wisdom. They pay 4 Silver, place the Scroll, gain 1 Influence in the Guild, gain 1 Gold, and move up 1 on a Research Track. That sounds fine, except Yellow only had 5 Silver total and now has almost nothing left. They also used their Travel slot when they could have used both Dice together for a much stronger action.

walkthrough.title: What Went Wrong: Spending Too Freely
walkthrough.text: Yellow made two mistakes. First, Delivering costs 4 Silver, which is a huge expense in the early game when you only have 5-8 Silver. Delivering is powerful, but doing it before you have a reliable Silver income from Research Tracks and Rest effects leaves you broke. Second, Yellow only used 1 Die when they had 2 available. By placing both the White 4 and the Blue 2, Yellow could have had a Blue 6 action (or used a Blue Worker to boost the Blue Die). This would have been a much higher-value action with a colour bonus for crossing the Blue path. Always consider playing 2 Dice when possible for maximum value.

walkthrough.title: Red's Second Turn: Work with Translate
walkthrough.text: Red spots a Persian Scroll Card in the Green Guild of the House of Wisdom. They place an Action Card on their Translate Action Slot. For Translation, the action colour must match the Guild -- Red needs a Green action. They place a Blue 4 and a Yellow 5 from their hand. Two primary colours mix: Blue + Yellow = Green. The value does not matter for Translate, so Green 9 is perfect. Red checks the translation chain. They need to go from Persian to Arabic. They can use Yellow's Persian/Arabic Translator. Red places 1 Gold on Yellow's Translator and pays Yellow 1 Silver (since it is not Red's own Translator). Red takes the Scroll Card, places it above their Player Board, moves up 1 on the matching Research Track, and resolves the Scroll's effect.

walkthrough.title: Why That Was a Strong Play
walkthrough.text: Red scored an early Scroll Card, which is worth VP at game end. They also advanced a Research Track. Yes, Red had to pay Yellow 1 Gold (placed on Yellow's Translator) and 1 Silver, but that Gold is pushing Yellow's Translator toward Retirement too, which benefits Yellow. This is the core tension of Translation: using opponents' Translators costs you Silver and gives them Gold toward Retirement VP, but sometimes you need to accept that cost to grab a valuable Scroll.

walkthrough.title: Blue's Second Turn: The Mistake -- Ignoring the Bag
walkthrough.text: Blue places an Action Card and puts down their last Die: a Yellow 3. Blue takes the 1-plus Research option to gain 1 Worker and 1 Silver. That is fine, but now Blue has 0 Dice in hand. They cannot Work next turn -- they have no Dice to place. Blue will be forced to Rest on their next turn even though they only have 2 Action Cards on their board, leaving 3 unplayed Action Cards doing nothing this cycle. Blue should have been paying attention to their Die supply and planned a Rest turn before running completely dry.

walkthrough.title: What Went Wrong: Running Out of Dice
walkthrough.text: Managing your Dice supply is critical in Scholars of the South Tigris. You start each cycle with around 4 Dice in hand, and every Work turn uses 1 or 2 of them. If you burn through all your Dice too quickly, you are forced into an early Rest before you have built up enough Rest effects to make it worthwhile. The ideal pattern is to Work for 3 to 5 turns, building up Action Cards with Rest effects on your board, then Rest to harvest a big income. Blue Rested too early with too few cards on the board, wasting their Rest turn's potential.

walkthrough.title: Yellow's Third Turn: The Mistake -- Forgetting Workers
walkthrough.text: Yellow has a Blue 2 left. They want to Research on the Astronomy (Blue) track, which requires the 4-plus option. But Blue 2 is only a value-2 action -- not enough for the 4-plus threshold. Yellow sighs and takes the 1-plus option for 1 Worker and 1 Silver. But wait -- Yellow has Workers in their supply. If Yellow had placed a Blue Worker on the Blue 2 Die, it would have boosted it to a Blue 6, easily clearing the 4-plus threshold and even the 8-plus option (with no Gold cost). Yellow forgot that Workers can increase a matching-colour Die to 6.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Workers Are Your Secret Weapon
walkthrough.text: Workers are enormously powerful in this game. A matching-colour Worker turns any Die into a 6 -- turning a measly 1 into a mighty 6. A different-colour Worker changes the Die's colour entirely, opening up Research Tracks and Guild colours you otherwise could not access. Always check your Worker supply before committing to a low-value action. The combination of Workers and 2 Dice can reach the maximum action value of 12, which unlocks the strongest Research options and lets you Travel the full Map loop.

walkthrough.title: Red Rests Efficiently
walkthrough.text: Red has played 4 Action Cards and has 1 Die left. They decide to Rest. Step 1: Red draws the bottom card of the Scroll Draw Pile -- it is a 2 VP Syriac Scroll Card. The Neutral Marker moves 2 steps clockwise (skipping White Spots). Red checks: the Scroll Card adjacent to the Neutral Marker has no Influence on it, so it gets replaced and the new Scroll goes to the Map. Step 2: Red resolves Rest income left to right across their Player Board. Their Action Cards' blue-background effects trigger: they gain 1 Silver, draw 2 Dice from their Bag, and harvest the Physics (Red) Research Track at level 1 (gaining the level-0 even-space benefit). Step 3: Red returns all Dice from their board to their Bag. Step 4: Red collects all 4 Action Cards back to hand. Red is reloaded and ready for another cycle.

walkthrough.title: How Rest Income Compounds
walkthrough.text: Notice how Red's Rest was much more productive than Blue's forced early Rest would have been. With 4 Action Cards on the board, Red triggered 4 sets of Rest effects. The more Action Cards you play before Resting, the more income you get. This is why timing your Rest is so important -- Work as many turns as your Dice supply allows, place Action Cards with strong Rest effects, then Rest to harvest everything at once. Think of it as building up a hand of Rest bonuses that all pay off simultaneously.

walkthrough.title: Continue Playing
walkthrough.text: Play continues clockwise. Each player keeps choosing to Work or Rest on their turn. You will cycle through your Action Cards multiple times before the game ends. Keep building your translation chain by Employing Translators, advance your Research Tracks for income and VP, Travel to Deliver Scroll Cards to the House of Wisdom, and Translate those Scrolls for VP. Watch for Caliph Cards appearing from the Draw Pile -- they signal the game is approaching its end and give Guild controllers valuable Influence placements.

walkthrough.title: Key Takeaways
walkthrough.text: From this tutorial, remember three lessons from Yellow's mistakes. First, do not Deliver Scroll Cards before you have a Silver income to support the 4 Silver cost -- build your Research Tracks and Rest effects first. Second, always play 2 Dice when possible for maximum action value. Third, always check your Workers before settling for a low-value action -- a matching Worker turns any Die into a 6. And from Blue's mistake: manage your Dice supply carefully. Plan your Work-Rest cycle so that you Rest with a full board of Action Cards, not an empty hand of Dice.

walkthrough.title: Before You Begin
walkthrough.text:
Before you dive into a full game, here are three things that can help.

First -- check out General Tips for strategy basics and common pitfalls to avoid.

Second -- reference the Appendix for a quick guide to every action, resource, and game term.

And most importantly -- ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Have fun translating those ancient scrolls!

summary.title: Welcome to the Tutorial
bullets: Scripted tutorial with 3 players: Red, Blue, Yellow
Recommended player count: 3 (manageable while showing interaction)
Follow along even with a different player count

summary.title: Starting Position
bullets: Each player: 7 Action Cards, ~4 Dice in hand, Gold, Silver, Workers, Bag with Dice
Each owns 1 Starting Translator, has Goal Card with Workers + Die, Starting Scroll Card
All 6 Research Markers at level 0; turn order: Yellow, Red, Blue

summary.title: Yellow's First Turn: Work with Recruit
bullets: Yellow plays Action Card on Recruit Slot, places Yellow 5 Die
Employs a Persian/Arabic Translator: pays 2 Silver, flips card, places in Room, marks with Influence
Now has a direct Persian-to-Arabic translation chain

summary.title: Why That Was Smart
bullets: Employing early builds translation infrastructure
Persian/Arabic covers Tier 1 language in a single Translator
Owning Translators = no Silver fee when you use them; opponents pay you instead

summary.title: Red's First Turn: Work with Travel
bullets: Red places Green 5 (translucent Die) on Travel Slot
Moves 4 steps to a White Spot; crosses Green path for 2 Silver bonus
Draws 1 Die from Bag; resolves Action Card brown effect for 1 Worker

summary.title: Blue's First Turn: Work with Research
bullets: Blue plays Red 4 + White 2 = Red 6 action on Research Slot
Takes 4+ option: spends 1 Gold, moves up Physics (Red) track, gains 2 Silver
Early Research Track progress builds future Rest income

summary.title: Yellow's Second Turn: The Mistake
bullets: Yellow uses only White 4 to Travel and Deliver -- costs 4 of 5 Silver, leaves them broke
Could have used both Dice (White 4 + Blue 2) for a Blue 6 with colour bonus
Delivering before building Silver income is risky

summary.title: What Went Wrong: Spending Too Freely
bullets: Delivering costs 4 Silver -- devastating in the early game with 5-8 total Silver
Always play 2 Dice when possible for maximum value and colour bonuses
Build Silver income (Research Tracks, Rest effects) before expensive Deliveries

summary.title: Red's Second Turn: Work with Translate
bullets: Red mixes Blue 4 + Yellow 5 = Green 9 action on Translate Slot
Uses Yellow's Persian/Arabic Translator: places 1 Gold on it, pays Yellow 1 Silver
Takes Persian Scroll from Green Guild, advances Research Track

summary.title: Why That Was a Strong Play
bullets: Early Scroll Card = VP at game end
Research Track advancement builds income
Using opponents' Translators costs Silver but can be worth it for the Scroll

summary.title: Blue's Second Turn: The Mistake
bullets: Blue uses their last Die on a weak 1+ Research for 1 Worker + 1 Silver
Now has 0 Dice in hand -- forced to Rest next turn with only 2 Action Cards down
Wasted Rest potential by not having enough cards on board

summary.title: What Went Wrong: Running Out of Dice
bullets: Burning through Dice forces an early, unproductive Rest
Ideal: Work 3-5 turns, stack Action Cards with Rest effects, then Rest for big income
Track your remaining Dice and plan Rest timing around them

summary.title: Yellow's Third Turn: The Mistake
bullets: Yellow has a Blue 2 but needs 4+ for Research; settles for weak 1+ option
Forgot they have a Blue Worker that could boost the Blue 2 Die to a Blue 6
Blue 6 would qualify for the 4+ or even 8+ Research option

summary.title: Lesson: Workers Are Your Secret Weapon
bullets: Matching-colour Worker turns any Die into a 6
Different-colour Worker changes the Die's colour entirely
Always check Workers before accepting a low-value action
2 Dice + Workers can reach the maximum value of 12

summary.title: Red Rests Efficiently
bullets: Red Rests with 4 Action Cards on board -- triggers 4 sets of Rest effects
Draw Pile reveals Scroll, Neutral Marker moves, Scrolls shuffle on Map
Income: Silver, Dice draws, Research Track harvesting
Returns all Dice to Bag, collects all Action Cards back

summary.title: How Rest Income Compounds
bullets: More Action Cards on board = more Rest effects = bigger income
Time your Rest for maximum payout, not forced by empty hand
Blue-background Action Card effects only trigger during Rest

summary.title: Continue Playing
bullets: Clockwise turns continue: Work or Rest each turn
Employ Translators, Research, Travel/Deliver, Translate Scrolls
Watch for Caliph Cards signaling game end approaching

summary.title: Key Takeaways
bullets: Don't Deliver before you can afford 4 Silver comfortably
Always play 2 Dice when possible for stronger actions
Check Workers before settling for low-value actions
Plan Work-Rest cycles: Rest with a full board of Action Cards

summary.title: Before You Begin
bullets: Check out General Tips for strategy basics
Reference the Appendix for quick-reference on actions, resources, and terms
Ask your personal GM Guide anytime -- tap Q&A for instant answers during play

walkthrough.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
walkthrough.text: Before diving into strategy, here are the most common mistakes new players make. First, Delivering Scroll Cards too early before building Silver income -- that 4 Silver cost is punishing when you only have 5-8 total. Second, forgetting to use Workers to boost Dice values -- a matching-colour Worker turns any Die into a 6, which is a massive value swing. Third, Resting too early with only 1-2 Action Cards on the board, wasting your Rest income potential. Fourth, ignoring the Dice Sum scoring -- accumulating too many White Dice will tank your Dice Sum and cost you VP at end of game. Fifth, neglecting to Employ Translators, leaving you dependent on expensive opponent or Neutral Translators for every Translation.

walkthrough.title: Starting Resource Card Strategy
walkthrough.text: During the draft, choosing your Starting Translator and Resource Card is your first major decision. Look at the Resource Card bonuses from top to bottom: how many Dice will you start with, how much Gold and Silver, which Workers, and what extra bonus? A Resource Card that gives you early Research Track progress is extremely strong because it starts building your Rest income from turn one. Also consider the Starting Translator: does it cover a useful language pair? Does it help complete your Goal Card's first target (interacting with a specific Translator)? Drafting in reverse turn order means the first player picks last -- if you are first player, plan for a slightly weaker starting position but the advantage of always going first.

walkthrough.title: Work-Rest Cycle Management
walkthrough.text: The core rhythm of Scholars is the Work-Rest cycle. You Work for several turns, placing Action Cards and spending Dice, then Rest to harvest income and reload. The ideal cycle length is 4-5 Work turns before Resting. This gives you maximum Rest income from Action Card blue-background effects plus Research Track harvesting. Resting after only 1-2 Work turns wastes your Rest potential. Being forced to Rest because you ran out of Dice wastes the opportunity to choose when to Rest. Track your Dice supply: if you have 4 Dice, plan for 2-3 Work turns (using 1-2 Dice each). Draw Dice effects on Action Cards and from the Map help extend your cycles.

walkthrough.title: Research Track Priorities
walkthrough.text: Advancing on Research Tracks serves three purposes: immediate benefits on odd-numbered spaces, Rest income on even-numbered spaces, and end-game VP based on final position. Early in the game, prioritize getting at least one track to level 2 so you have a Rest income source. The Astronomy (Blue) track is always harvested during Rest because it is printed on your Player Aid -- focus on this one for guaranteed income every Rest. Getting any track to level 3 triggers a Goal Card target. Levels 5-7 are worth 3-5 VP each at game end, making Research Tracks one of the most efficient scoring paths.

walkthrough.title: Managing Your Dice Bag
walkthrough.text: Your Bag is your engine in Scholars, and what goes into it matters enormously. Coloured Dice are valuable because they increase your Dice Sum at game end and give you access to specific Research Tracks and Guilds. Secondary Dice (translucent, values 4-6 only) are particularly strong because they never roll below 4 and take priority over primary colours. White Dice are a double-edged sword: they are the most common and easiest to get, but every White Die reduces your Dice Sum. The Starting Scroll Card penalizes you 3 VP if you have 6 or more White Dice. Be strategic about gaining White Dice -- sometimes the Retirement cost of gaining a White Die is worth it, but accumulating too many will hurt you at game end.

walkthrough.title: Translation Chain Planning
walkthrough.text: Building an efficient translation chain is key to scoring Scroll Card VP. Plan your Translator employment around the languages available in the House of Wisdom. Tier 1 languages (Persian and Syriac) only need 1 Translator to reach Arabic. Tier 2 (Greek and Hebrew) need at least 2. Tier 3 (Chinese and Sanskrit) need at least 3. Owning Translators saves you 1 Silver per Translation and prevents opponents from benefiting off your Gold placements. Translators who can skip tiers or speak multiple languages in the same tier are extremely valuable -- they shorten your chains and reduce Gold costs.

walkthrough.title: Goal Card Completion
walkthrough.text: Your Goal Card has 3 individual targets that can be completed in any order. The first is to interact with a specific Translator (Dispatch, Employ, Translate through, Retire, or place/take Gold). The second is to Translate a Scroll from a specific Guild. The third is to reach level 3 on a specific Research Track. Completing all 3 flips the Goal Card into a new Action Card with a pre-printed White 5 Die and unlocks a 6th Action Slot. This is a meaningful power spike: an extra slot means one more Work turn per cycle and one more Rest income source. Aim to complete your Goal Card by mid-game if possible.

walkthrough.title: Guild Control and Influence
walkthrough.text: Controlling Guilds pays off in two ways: VP from the Guild itself at game end, and the ability to place Influence on Caliph Cards when they are revealed (choosing between 3 VP, 1 VP plus a Die, or Retiring a Translator). The first player to place Influence in an empty Guild takes control for free. Later, you need more Influence than the current controller to take over. Influence is limited to 20 per player, so spreading it thin across Scrolls, Guilds, and Translators can leave you short. Focus your Influence on 1-2 Guilds for control rather than sprinkling it everywhere. The consolation reward for non-controlling players with Influence in a Guild (1 Worker, 1 Silver, or 1 Gold) is nice but far less valuable than the VP from controlling.

summary.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
bullets: Don't Deliver before you can afford 4 Silver comfortably
Don't forget Workers -- matching-colour Worker turns any Die to 6
Don't Rest with only 1-2 Action Cards down -- waste of Rest income
Watch your White Dice count -- 6+ costs you 3 VP from Starting Scroll
Don't neglect Employing Translators -- relying on opponents is expensive

summary.title: Starting Resource Card Strategy
bullets: Evaluate Resource Card bonuses: Dice count, Gold, Silver, Workers, Research Track advances
Cards granting early Research Track progress = strong Rest income from turn 1
Consider Starting Translator language coverage and Goal Card synergy
First player drafts last but always goes first -- weaker start, persistent tempo advantage

summary.title: Work-Rest Cycle Management
bullets: Ideal cycle: 4-5 Work turns, then Rest for maximum income harvest
Resting early (1-2 cards down) wastes potential
Track your Dice supply: 4 Dice = plan for 2-3 Work turns
Use Dice-draw Action Card effects and Map spots to extend cycles

summary.title: Research Track Priorities
bullets: Get at least 1 track to level 2 early for Rest income
Astronomy (Blue) is always harvested during Rest -- prioritize it
Level 3 = Goal Card target; Levels 5-7 = 3-5 VP each at game end
Odd spaces = immediate benefit; even spaces = Rest income

summary.title: Managing Your Dice Bag
bullets: Coloured Dice increase Dice Sum (= VP ranking at game end)
Secondary Dice (translucent): minimum value 4 + secondary colour priority = very strong
White Dice reduce Dice Sum; 6+ White Dice = -3 VP from Starting Scroll
Be selective about gaining White Dice as Retirement costs

summary.title: Translation Chain Planning
bullets: Tier 1 (Persian, Syriac): 1 Translator to Arabic; Tier 2 (Greek, Hebrew): 2; Tier 3 (Chinese, Sanskrit): 3
Own your Translators to save Silver and deny opponent income
Tier-skipping and multi-language Translators are very valuable
Plan Employments around languages available in the House of Wisdom

summary.title: Goal Card Completion
bullets: 3 targets: interact with a specific Translator, Translate from a specific Guild, reach level 3 on a track
Can be completed in any order
Completing all 3 flips Goal Card into Action Card with White 5 Die + unlocks 6th Action Slot
Aim for mid-game completion for maximum benefit

summary.title: Guild Control and Influence
bullets: Control = VP at game end + Influence placement on Caliph Cards (3 VP, 1 VP+Die, or Retire)
First player to place in empty Guild takes control for free
Need more Influence than controller to take over
Focus on 1-2 Guilds rather than spreading 20 Influence thin

walkthrough.title: The Translator Engine
walkthrough.text: One of the strongest strategies is to heavily invest in Employing Translators. Every Translator you own serves triple duty: it reduces your Translation costs (no Silver fee), it generates VP when Retired (printed VP on the card plus Retiree action bonuses), and it forces opponents to pay you Silver when they use it. The ideal engine has 3-4 Employed Translators covering a wide language chain from Tier 3 all the way to Arabic. This lets you Translate expensive Tier 3 Scrolls (Chinese and Sanskrit) that other players struggle with, earning high VP Scroll Cards while your opponents are limited to easy Tier 1 translations.

walkthrough.title: Retirement Timing
walkthrough.text: Retiring Translators is both a scoring opportunity and a strategic decision about when to give up a Translator. Each Retired Translator goes under one of your 4 brown Action Slots and provides a permanent Retiree effect that triggers every time you use that Action Slot. Some Retirees let you move up Research Tracks by spending a Worker. Others increase your main action's value by up to 4. Others provide alternative actions. Stack multiple Retirees under the Action Slot you use most frequently. The first Retiree under each slot is free; subsequent ones cost 1 Silver each or gain 1 White Die to Bag. Time your Retirements so that you push Gold onto Translators you want to Retire while avoiding pushing opponents' Translators over the threshold.

walkthrough.title: Dice Sum Optimization
walkthrough.text: The Dice Sum score at game end can be worth 0-8 VP depending on your rank among all players. Your Dice Sum equals total coloured Dice minus total White Dice. To maximize this, aggressively acquire coloured Dice (especially secondary Dice with guaranteed high values) and minimize White Dice. Destroy White Dice whenever you can through Action Card effects that spend Silver or Workers to Destroy White Dice. Avoid gaining White Dice as Retirement costs unless the Retiree's benefit is worth the Dice Sum penalty. Remember your Starting Scroll Card: 6+ White Dice costs you 3 VP on top of the Dice Sum loss.

walkthrough.title: Research Track Racing
walkthrough.text: Reaching level 7 on a Research Track is worth 5 VP and also provides the overflow benefit (Influence in a Guild or Gold) every time you would advance further. Getting multiple tracks to level 5-7 can easily net 15-25 VP from Research alone. The 10-plus Research option (spend 2 Gold, move 2 spaces) is the most efficient way to race up a track, but requires a coloured 10+ action -- achievable with 2 Dice and Workers. Plan your colour access around the Research Tracks you want to push. If you are investing in Physics (Red), make sure you have Red Dice and Red Workers available for 8+ or 10+ Research actions.

walkthrough.title: Scroll Card VP Stacking
walkthrough.text: Translated Scroll Cards score VP at game end based on various conditions. Some score for matching coloured Dice in your collection. Some score for the number of Scroll Cards you have Translated in specific languages. Some score for Research Track positions. The key insight is that if multiple Scrolls share the same scoring condition, they each score the full amount independently. This means doubling or tripling down on a specific scoring condition -- like having many Purple Dice, or multiple Scrolls that reward Persian language translations -- creates exponential returns. When choosing which Scrolls to Translate, prioritize those whose conditions you are already building toward.

walkthrough.title: Caliph Card Manipulation
walkthrough.text: Since Caliph Cards are shuffled into the Draw Pile in a structured way, experienced players can roughly predict when they will appear. Each middle stack of the Draw Pile contains exactly 1 Caliph Card. By tracking how many Scrolls have been drawn from each section, you can anticipate upcoming Caliph revelations. Before a Caliph Card appears, make sure you control the Guilds where you want the 3 VP top-space placement. If you cannot control a Guild, having at least 1 Influence in it guarantees you the consolation reward (Worker, Silver, or Gold). Timing a Caliph revelation through Delivering gives you more control than waiting for it to appear during someone else's Rest turn.

walkthrough.title: Influence Economy
walkthrough.text: With only 20 Influence, every placement must be intentional. Influence on Translators is locked until Retirement. Influence on Scroll Cards provides a 1 Silver toll but is returned after interaction. Influence in Guilds scores VP through control and Caliph Card placement. Influence on Caliph Cards is permanent VP. The most impactful Influence placements are Guild control (ongoing benefit across multiple Caliph revelations) and Caliph Card top spaces (3 VP each). Avoid placing Influence on low-value Scroll Cards where the 1 Silver toll does not offset losing the Influence. Late-game, count your remaining Influence and plan exactly where each one goes for maximum VP.

walkthrough.title: Colour Flexibility
walkthrough.text: Advanced players maintain colour flexibility to adapt to changing board states. Having a mix of primary and secondary Dice in your Bag means you can access any Research Track and any Guild colour. Workers provide additional colour manipulation. The most flexible position is having at least 1 Die in each primary colour plus some secondary Dice and a stock of coloured Workers. This lets you Translate from any Guild, Research on any track, and take colour bonuses on any Map path. Conversely, being locked into a single colour limits your options dramatically. When drafting Dice through Action Card effects and Research Track bonuses, spread your colour access rather than concentrating.

walkthrough.title: Opponent Interaction
walkthrough.text: Scholars has more player interaction than it first appears. Every Translation using an opponent's Translator gives them Gold toward Retirement and costs you Silver. Placing Influence on Map Scroll Cards creates a toll for opponents wanting to Deliver. Guild control is a direct competition. Even the Rest action affects all players through Neutral Marker movement and Scroll Card reshuffling. Watch your opponents' translation chains -- if they are building toward a specific Tier 3 language, consider Employing Translators that cover those languages so you earn Silver when they Translate. Block Guild control by placing just enough Influence to prevent opponents from gaining the 3 VP Caliph Card spaces.

walkthrough.title: End-Game Optimization
walkthrough.text: When the 3rd Caliph Card appears, the end is near. You have the current Round plus the next one after the 4th Caliph appears. In the final rounds, shift from engine-building to point-maximizing. Translate high-VP Scrolls even if the chain is expensive. Push Research Tracks from level 4 to 5 (the jump from 2 VP to 3 VP) or from level 6 to 7 (4 VP to 5 VP). Secure Guild control before the final Caliph Card. Clean up your Dice Sum by destroying any remaining White Dice. Convert Silver to Gold through your Starting Scroll Card (every 2 Silver becomes 1 Gold at game end, and every 2 Gold scores 1 VP). Do not leave unspent resources on the table.

summary.title: The Translator Engine
bullets: Employ 3-4 Translators covering a chain from Tier 3 to Arabic
Own your Translators: no Silver fee, earn Silver from opponents, VP on Retirement
Wide language coverage lets you Translate expensive Tier 3 Scrolls others cannot

summary.title: Retirement Timing
bullets: Retired Translators tuck under brown Action Slots for permanent effects
Stack Retirees under your most-used Action Slot for compounding benefit
First Retiree per slot = free; additional = 1 Silver or 1 White Die each
Control Gold placement to time your Retirements, not opponents'

summary.title: Dice Sum Optimization
bullets: Dice Sum = coloured Dice - White Dice; ranked for 0-8 VP
Acquire coloured Dice aggressively, especially secondary (translucent) Dice
Destroy White Dice via Action Card effects when possible
6+ White Dice = -3 VP from Starting Scroll on top of Dice Sum loss

summary.title: Research Track Racing
bullets: Level 7 = 5 VP per track + overflow benefit on future advances
10+ Research (2 Gold, 2 spaces) is the most efficient climbing option
Plan colour access around target tracks: match Dice and Workers to track colours
Multiple tracks at 5-7 can net 15-25 VP from Research alone

summary.title: Scroll Card VP Stacking
bullets: Multiple Scrolls with same scoring condition each score full amount independently
Doubling/tripling down on one condition creates exponential returns
Translate Scrolls that reward what you are already building toward
Conditions include: matching Dice colour, language count, Research Track level, Guild Influence

summary.title: Caliph Card Manipulation
bullets: Each middle Draw Pile stack has exactly 1 Caliph Card -- track draws to predict timing
Secure Guild control before Caliph reveals for 3 VP top-space placement
At minimum, have 1 Influence in Guilds for consolation rewards
Delivering to trigger Caliph gives you more control than waiting for Rest reveals

summary.title: Influence Economy
bullets: 20 Influence total per player -- every placement must be intentional
Highest-value placements: Guild control (ongoing) and Caliph Card top spaces (3 VP each)
Avoid low-value Scroll Card placements where 1 Silver toll is not worth the locked Influence
Late game: count remaining Influence and plan each placement for maximum VP

summary.title: Colour Flexibility
bullets: Maintain at least 1 Die in each primary colour + some secondary Dice
Workers add colour manipulation -- stock 2-3 different colours
Flexibility = access to any Research Track, Guild, and Map colour path
Spread colour access rather than concentrating in one colour

summary.title: Opponent Interaction
bullets: Using opponents' Translators gives them Gold toward Retirement + costs you Silver
Influence on Map Scrolls creates tolls; Guild control is direct competition
Employ Translators in popular languages to earn Silver from opponents' Translations
Block Guild control with targeted Influence placement

summary.title: End-Game Optimization
bullets: After 3rd Caliph: shift from engine-building to pure point-maximizing
Push Research Tracks to next VP threshold (4->5, 6->7 especially valuable)
Secure Guild control before final Caliph; clean up Dice Sum
Convert Silver to Gold via Starting Scroll (2 Silver -> 1 Gold -> VP at 2 Gold per VP)

entries.definition: One of the two turn options. Play 1 Action Card onto an empty Action Slot, place 1-2 Dice on it, optionally place Workers to modify Dice, then resolve actions.
One of the two turn options. Requires at least 1 Action Card on the Player Board. Reveal bottom Scroll from Draw Pile, gain income left to right, return Dice to Bag, return Action Cards to hand.

Cards played from hand onto Action Slots when Working. Each has blue-background Rest effects (resolved when Resting) and may have brown-background immediate effects. Players start with 7 Action Cards.

Spaces on the Player Board where Action Cards are placed. Each slot provides 1 main action (Recruit, Travel, Research, or Translate). Players start with 5 slots; a 6th is unlocked by completing the Goal Card.
One of 4 main actions. Dispatch or Employ 1 Translator from the top of the Main Board. Colour does not matter; only the action's value determines which Translators are accessible.

One of 4 main actions. Move your Player Marker clockwise around the Map's 12 Spots. Must move at least 1 step. White Spots give small benefits; Black Spots allow Delivering Scrolls to the House of Wisdom.
One of 4 main actions. Choose from 4 options (1+, 4+, 8+, 10+) to gain Workers/Silver and advance on Research Tracks. Higher options require specific colour and higher action value.

One of 4 main actions. Use Employed Translators to convert a Scroll Card in the House of Wisdom from its original language to Arabic. Action colour must match the Guild. Place 1 Gold on each Translator used.
A Recruit sub-action. Resolve the effects in the top-right corner of a Translator Card for free, then return it to the bottom of its Deck. Does not cost Silver or place Influence.

A Recruit sub-action. Pay 0-4 Silver to place a Translator into an available Room along the bottom of the Main Board. Flip the card over, resolve the Room's immediate effects, and place 1 Influence on the Translator.

A Travel sub-action at Black Spots. Pick up the adjacent Map Scroll Card, place it in the House of Wisdom, pay 4 Silver, gain 1-2 Guild Influence, gain 1 Gold (+ 1 White Die if lowest row), advance 1 on matching Research Track, reveal top Draw Pile card.

Happens when Gold on a Translator reaches the Room's required number. Employed: return Gold, return Influence to owner, tuck card under a brown Action Slot. Neutral: return Gold + card to Deck. Only happens at end of current player's turn.

Cards representing linguists who translate between languages. 64 regular cards in 8 Decks of 8, plus 10 Starting Translators. Each shows which language pair(s) it covers. Employed Translators sit in Rooms; Retired ones tuck under Action Slots.
One of 10 special Translator Cards used during setup. 4 are revealed and placed into Rooms. Players draft one each; unclaimed ones become Neutral Translators.
A Translator in a Room without any player's Influence on it. Using a Neutral Translator costs 1 Silver to the Main Supply. When Retired, its card returns to the bottom of its Deck.

Cards representing foreign manuscripts. 48 total in the game. Found on the Map (6 spaces) and in the House of Wisdom (up to 9 spaces). Each has a language, a VP value, a Research Track type, and may have immediate effects and end-game scoring conditions.
Each player's personal Scroll Card received during setup. At game end: converts every 2 Silver to 1 Gold, scores 1 VP per 2 Gold, and loses 3 VP if you have 6 or more White Dice.

4 special cards shuffled into the Draw Pile. When revealed, they trigger events (Gold on Starting Translators, Neutral Translator added) and let Guild controllers place Influence on VP reward spaces. All 4 revealed = game end triggered.

1 per player, placed on the right-most Player Board space. Has 3 targets: interact with a specific Translator, Translate from a specific Guild, reach level 3 on a specific track. Completing all 3 flips it into an Action Card with a pre-printed White 5 Die and unlocks a 6th Action Slot.
Drafted during setup alongside Starting Translators. Provides starting Dice, Gold, Silver, Workers, and bonus effects. After resolving, flipped over to serve as a Player Aid for the rest of the game.
Used during setup to construct the Scroll Card Draw Pile in 6 stacks based on player count. Returned to the box after the Draw Pile is built.
Spaces along the bottom of the Main Board where Employed Translators are placed. Each Room shows: Silver cost (0-4), immediate effects when placing, and Gold requirement for Retirement.

The circular path on the Main Board with 12 Spots. White Spots provide small benefits; Black Spots allow Delivering Scrolls. 6 adjacent spaces hold Map Scroll Cards. Coloured paths between spots provide bonuses when crossed.
The area on the Main Board with 9 Card spaces arranged in 3 Guild columns of 3 rows each. Scroll Cards are Delivered here from the Map and Translated from here by players. Guilds fill top to bottom.

One of 3 columns in the House of Wisdom, each with a coloured Minaret: Purple, Orange, and Green (left to right). Players compete for control by placing Influence. Controllers place Influence on Caliph Cards and score VP at game end.
The top of each Guild column, showing the Guild's secondary colour. The controlling player's Influence sits in the outlined space at the top; other players' Influence sits in the open area below.

6 tracks on the Main Board, levels 0-7. Physics (Red), Mathematics (Purple), Astronomy (Blue), Chemistry (Orange), Philosophy (Yellow), Biology (Green). Odd levels = immediate benefit; even levels = Rest income.
One of the 6 Research Tracks, coloured red. Uses the Physics icon. Advances when Translating or Delivering Red-type Scrolls, or via Research actions with a Red colour.
One of the 6 Research Tracks, coloured purple. Uses the Mathematics icon.
One of the 6 Research Tracks, coloured blue. Uses the Astronomy icon. Always harvested during Rest because it is printed on the Player Aid.
One of the 6 Research Tracks, coloured orange. Uses the Chemistry icon.
One of the 6 Research Tracks, coloured yellow. Uses the Philosophy icon.
One of the 6 Research Tracks, coloured green. Uses the Biology icon.
Smaller circular spots on the Map. Provide minor benefits when you land there, such as gaining Silver or drawing Dice from your Bag.
Larger circular spots on the Map. Landing here allows you to Deliver the adjacent Scroll Card to the House of Wisdom for 4 Silver.
End-game calculation: total coloured Dice minus total White Dice across all Dice you own (supply, Player Board, Bag). Players are ranked on the Dice Sum banner on the Main Board for VP.
The black banner at the top of the Main Board. Players place their Player Markers from lowest to highest Dice Sum. Position determines VP earned. Tied players share VP from their combined positions.
Standard 6-sided die with faces 1-6. White Dice are the most common but reduce your Dice Sum at game end. Having 6+ White Dice costs 3 VP from the Starting Scroll Card.
Standard 6-sided dice in Red, Blue, or Yellow with faces 1-6. Opaque like White Dice. Primary Dice are limited; if unavailable, you may return a White Die to the supply instead.
Translucent dice in Purple, Orange, or Green with faces 4, 4, 5, 5, 6, 6 only. Minimum roll of 4 makes them very strong. Secondary colours take priority over primary when determining action colour.

Each player's personal cloth bag holding their Dice pool. Dice are drawn randomly and rolled. When empty during a draw, return all Dice from the Player Board (except current action) to the Bag, then continue.
White Workers. Can be placed on any Die: same-colour (White) Die = increase to 6; different-colour Die = change to White. Also spent as costs for various card effects.
Workers in Red, Blue, or Yellow. Place on a Die: matching colour = increase to 6; different colour = change Die colour. Cannot place 2 coloured Workers on 1 Die to create secondary colour.

Premium currency. Used to pay for Research (4+ and 10+ options), placed on Translators during Translation (1 Gold per Translator used), and scores VP at game end via Starting Scroll (1 VP per 2 Gold). 35 in the supply.

Common currency. Used to Employ Translators (0-4 cost), Deliver Scrolls (4 cost), pay opponents for using their Translators (1 each), and various Action Card effects. 50 in the supply. Converts to Gold at game end via Starting Scroll.

20 tokens per player in their colour. Used to mark Translator ownership, claim Scroll Cards, control Guilds, and occupy Caliph Card reward spaces. Limited; if supply is empty, may relocate from a Scroll Card or Guild (not a Translator).

Each player's personal board with 5 Action Slots (4 brown, 1 black, plus Goal Card slot). Action Cards are placed here when Working. Rest effects are resolved left to right. Retirees tuck under brown Action Slots.
The reverse side of the Resource Card. Shows the language tier chart, the Rest sequence reminder, and the Astronomy (Blue) Research Track harvesting that occurs every Rest.
1 per player, placed on the Map during play. Moves clockwise during Travel actions. Also used on the Dice Sum banner during final scoring.

Single marker placed on the Map. Moves clockwise during Rest (skipping White Spots, steps equal to revealed Scroll VP value). Determines which Scroll Cards may be displaced or delivered to the House of Wisdom.
6 per player, one on each Research Track. Tracks progress from level 0 to 7. Position determines Rest income and end-game VP (1 VP at levels 1-2, 2 VP at 3-4, 3 VP at 5, 4 VP at 6, 5 VP at 7).
Given to the randomly selected first player during setup. Stays with the same player for the entire game (does not rotate). The player to their right takes the last turn of the game.
The target language of all translations. Considered Tier 0. Every translation chain must end at Arabic. Some Translators go directly to Arabic from Tier 1 languages.
A Tier 1 language. Requires only 1 Translator to reach Arabic. Found on some Scroll Cards in the House of Wisdom.
A Tier 1 language. Requires only 1 Translator to reach Arabic.
A Tier 2 language. Requires at least 2 Translators to reach Arabic (unless a tier-skipping Translator is available).
A Tier 2 language. Requires at least 2 Translators to reach Arabic.
A Tier 3 language. Requires at least 3 Translators to reach Arabic. Scroll Cards in this language are typically harder to Translate but may offer higher VP.
A Tier 3 language. Requires at least 3 Translators to reach Arabic.

Two primary Dice combine to form a secondary colour: Red + Blue = Purple, Red + Yellow = Orange, Blue + Yellow = Green. Secondary Dice take priority over primary. If both secondary colours are possible, the player chooses.

Remove a White Die permanently from your Bag, hand, or Player Board. Several Action Card effects allow this, often at the cost of Silver or Workers. Reduces your White Die count for Dice Sum and Starting Scroll scoring.

During Rest, gain the benefit of the highest even-numbered space your Research Marker has reached on a given track. If on an odd number, harvest the even space below it. Each track is only harvested if its associated Action Card is on the Player Board (except Astronomy/Blue, which is always harvested).

Effects printed on Action Cards in blue. These only trigger when the player Rests, resolved left to right across the Player Board. Include gaining Dice, Silver, Gold, Workers, and harvesting Research Tracks.

Effects printed on Action Cards in brown. Resolved immediately on the turn the card is played, in any order alongside the main action. Include gaining Workers, drawing Dice, Influencing Scroll Cards, and placing/taking Gold on Translators.

Permanent effects gained from Retired Translators tucked under Action Slots. Trigger when using that Action Slot. Effects include Research Track advances (spend 1 Worker), action value increases (up to +4), alternative main actions, and treating White Dice as a specific colour.

The face-down pile of Scroll and Caliph Cards built during setup. Cards are drawn from the top (when Delivering) and from the bottom (when Resting). Structured in 6 stacks with Caliph Cards distributed in the middle 4.
A shorter game variant that removes some Scroll Cards from the Draw Pile. Recommended for first-time players and higher player counts. No rule changes -- just fewer cards.
The full-length game with the maximum number of Scroll Cards in the Draw Pile. Provides a longer, deeper experience.
When Caliph Cards are resolved, non-controlling players who have at least 1 Influence in a Guild gain a small reward: 1 Worker, 1 Silver, or 1 Gold, as shown along the bottom of the Minaret.

A player controls a Guild when they have more Influence in it than any other player. The controlling player's Influence sits at the top of the Minaret. Control grants VP at game end and the right to place Influence on Caliph Cards.
End-game scoring based on final Research Marker positions: Levels 1-2 = 1 VP, Levels 3-4 = 2 VP, Level 5 = 3 VP, Level 6 = 4 VP, Level 7 = 5 VP per track.

If total VP is tied, the player with the most Employed Translators still on the Main Board wins. If still tied, the player with the most Workers in supply wins. If still tied, players share the victory.
